title: .buildkit_qemu_emulator: operation not supported
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/23890
Originally posted by **anthr76** September 6, 2024 Hello! I'm trying to use the Docker CLI with the `DOCKER_HOST` set to the Podman socket on MacOS. I have a project I'm trying to build (https://github.com/goauthentik/authentik) that uses buildx. I'm using a arm64 Mac and the command I'm trying to invoke is `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build . --tag authentik:test1 --platform=linux/amd64 -f Dockerfile --load` This fails with a error ``` /dev/.buildkit_qemu_emulator ... : did not complete successfully: failed to copy xattrs: failed to set xattr "security.selinux" on /tmp/buildkit-qemu-emulator2534885165/dev/.buildkit_qemu_emulator: operation not supported ``` This does run okay on the native architecture. Has anyone ever seen this before and know if it's a bug or a workaround? I found this related issue https://github.com/dagger/dagger/issues/8054 I know disabling SELinux on CoreOS is usually not a route you can really take so not sure how to approach this.
Does anyone know which codepath this lies in?

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Moving a comment over from #24646:

A partial explanation of what is happening, in my understanding:

1. Podman's machine VM, which runs [Fedora CoreOS](https://fedoraproject.org/coreos/), ships with [SELinux](https://www.redhat.com/en/topics/linux/what-is-selinux) enabled.

2. When using `docker buildx build`, a [BuildKit](https://github.com/moby/buildkit) container is started on the machine VM to build the desired container image. This container provides the emulator `buildkit-qemu-x86_64` (say), which is used to execute foreign-architecture binaries during the build process.

3. The `buildkit-qemu-x86_64` binary has a `security.selinux` extended attribute:

        $ docker exec -it buildx_buildkit_default /bin/sh -c 'apk add attr && getfattr -d -m "" -- $(command -v buildkit-qemu-x86_64)'     
        ...
        security.selinux="unconfined_u:object_r:container_ro_file_t:s0"

4. When BuildKit prepares the build, it *copies* `buildkit-qemu-x86_64` to a temporary location which is then mounted into the building container ([source](https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/solver/llbsolver/ops/exec_binfmt.go)).

    This copy operation attempts to duplicate all extended attributes. However, SELinux denies this in order to protect the `security.selinux` access control policy. BuildKit treats the failure to copy the xattr as fatal.

    BuildKit could ignore this error, which was previously done elsewhere in the Docker ecosystem. https://github.com/moby/buildkit/issues/5544

As a workaround, you can disable SELinux on the machine VM.

<details>
<summary>I solemnly swear I will not do this in production.</summary>

1. Enter the machine VM with `podman machine ssh` and edit `/etc/sysconfig/selinux` to set `SELINUX=disabled`.

2. Restart the machine VM with `podman machine stop && podman machine start`.

3. Ensure there are no lingering BuildKit containers -- existing ones won't work after SELinux policy has changed. Clear them out with `docker ps -a` and `docker rm -f <id>`.

4. Clear the Docker build cache for good measure: `docker buildx prune`
</details>
@Sayed-Imran the `multiarch/qemu-user-static` is only published for linux/amd64 and would not work on arm64 podman host.

> ~~`docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes`~~

I do not recommend this command. This is one of the not-solutions that crops up in many discussions of the issue but I think it's distracting from the root cause. Please, **let's consider further discussion of this command as off-topic for this issue** and continue elsewhere.

<details>
<summary>Why not to use `multiarch/qemu-user-static`</summary>

---

Firstly, this is an outdated container image that that was last pushed *2 years ago* and contains old copies of the `qemu-user-static` emulators.

The BuildKit container provides its own, newer `qemu-user-static`. And, so does the Podman machine VM.

```
% podman machine ssh -- find /usr/bin -iname '*qemu*'
/usr/bin/qemu-i386-static
/usr/bin/qemu-x86_64-static
/usr/bin/qemu-aarch64-static
```

As @kaovilai points out, this old `multiarch/qemu-user-static` is not really multi-arch, because it's only published for `linux/amd64`, and it can't be run natively on other architectures (e.g.., Apple Silicon).

The container's entry point writes to `/proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/register` to register these emulators with the [`binfmt_misc` kernel feature](https://docs.kernel.org/admin-guide/binfmt-misc.html). This affects the container host, which is why `--privileged` is required, and persists until reboot. This will cause unexpected behavior if the container host already had a different version of QEMU installed, which then gets overridden by this container.

Although it's a neat trick that you can do this, it's a much better idea to just configure your container host properly so that the change persists across reboots. Use your distro's (probably newer) packaged QEMU. Don't let weird container images reconfigure your kernel.

Again, if you are running on macOS or Windows, the Podman machine VM is already properly configured.

---

</details>

As I wrote in my earlier comment, the issue has to do with SELinux denying an attempt to set an extended attribute on a file, which BuildKit treats as a fatal error but shouldn't. It has basically nothing to do with the particular binary in question and any discussion of emulation, `binfmt_misc`, cross-architecture anything is basically a red herring.

I experienced a similar error message with building amd64 container using docker buildx with podman on M3 MacBook Pro. The following command to install qemu manually worked for me.

```
podman run -it --rm --privileged tonistiigi/binfmt --install all
```

Installing qemu manually is mentioned in the docker docs.
https://docs.docker.com/build/building/multi-platform/#install-qemu-manually

On the other hand, while looking thru github, there are warnings not to disable selinux.
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14376#issuecomment-1600964041

> I experienced a similar error message with building amd64 container using docker buildx with podman on M3 MacBook Pro. The following command to install qemu manually worked for me.
> 
> ```
> podman run -it --rm --privileged tonistiigi/binfmt --install all
> ```
> 
> Installing qemu manually is mentioned in the docker docs. https://docs.docker.com/build/building/multi-platform/#install-qemu-manually
> 
> On the other hand, while looking thru github, there are warnings not to disable selinux. 
> [#14376 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14376#issuecomment-1600964041)

This solution I tried ended up giving me issues using docker compose to run any containers. Every command lead to segmentation fault without any stdout. I suspected it was due to rosetta misconfiguration.
https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22471

https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/rosetta
I ended up disabling rosetta and recreated podman machine. This allowed me to run and build amd64 and arm64 containers without any issue.

The `security.selinux` xattr error happens because the shared QEMU VM's filesystem doesn't support the xattrs BuildKit needs during emulated builds. This is a fundamental limitation of the shared-VM + virtfs approach.

We built **[mocker](https://github.com/us/mocker)** specifically to avoid this: it uses Apple's Containerization framework (`Virtualization.framework`) instead of QEMU — one native Linux VM per container, no shared filesystem layer, no xattr issues. The Docker/Podman socket compatibility layer (Docker Engine API v1.47 + Podman libpod API v5.0.0) just landed: https://github.com/us/mocker/pull/8

If you're on macOS 26 Apple Silicon, this might be worth trying as an alternative. No QEMU involved anywhere.

> [!Note]
> Responses generated with Claude
This can also probably closed since https://github.com/moby/buildkit/pull/6015 merged

@rgov there are alternatives that I have found out since then. https://github.com/socktainer/socktainer achieves what I had wanted to do and more. So I will probably switch to contributing there instead.

mocker and podman both wants to be its own CLI for the most part. I do not have any ins/benefit in promoting any particular project. Just a frustrated user that have fixed things on my own cause its not priotized by any of the projects I use.

And I work for the company that majorly contributes to podman so I do want podman to succeed.

When contributing in opensource, it's always good to share examples of others even if its architecture is impossible to replicate in the source discussion project because it can inspire alternatives.

Framing any responses that in fact discloses any AI / translator use as slop is just not helpful to anyone.

I will still have to use podman + qemu because I actually sometimes have to build for platforms (ppc64 and s390x) that are neither supported by apple/containers (which is used by referenced mocker/socktainer above).

However there remains some race condition open issues for users to be aware of, which is https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/work_items/2738 seems to be unsolved.

The xttr support claim is true and has been reviewed by buildkit in PRs above. It is a problem that is specifically for people who NEEDS docker cli without Docker Desktop, or reusing podman exposed socket with docker cli via a buildx which uses buildkit code that had issues prior to my fix to buildkit above.
If anyone is able to validate/crosscheck if https://github.com/moby/buildkit/pull/6015 is shipped/effective in latest docker cli that would be awesome so we can close out this thread.
